title: The Top-Down
energy: Confident, slightly fast — you're showing your method
setup: Standing at whiteboard at Orage HQ. Marker in hand. Camera positioned to capture both you and the board.
shotDirections: Camera at chest height, locked tripod
Frame: half whiteboard, half you
Window light from camera-left
Wear collared shirt, no logo

sections.action: Sketch a simple pyramid fast on whiteboard. Top: 'FOUNDER.' Middle: 'LEADERSHIP.' Bottom: 'TEAM.' Then circle the top hard with the marker.
Turn to camera. Marker still in hand.
spokenLines: Most AI agencies start at the bottom. We start here.

sections.action: Back at the board. Gesture as you talk. Tap items.
spokenLines: We look at what the founder is doing every morning.
The dashboards he needs but doesn't have.
The Excel sheets eating his Tuesdays.
The inbox he should never be touching.
We take that off his plate first. Then we work down.

sections.action: Back to direct camera. Marker at side.
spokenLines: If you're the founder still doing leadership work AND admin work — book a free audit. Link below.

title: Would Your Next Hire Eat All Your Profit?
energy: Direct, slightly intense — financial reality talk
setup: Behind desk at Orage HQ. Direct to camera. Notebook with rough numbers visible.
shotDirections: Eye-level camera
Soft window light from camera-right
Slight lean-forward energy on the hook

spokenLines: Would your next hire eat all your profit? You know what you need to grow — but that step would also make you starve.

sections.action: Stay on you. Conversational but pointed.
spokenLines: I see it all the time. You're between one and three million dollars.
You're growing. But that next step feels like a risk.
You spend on ads — they don't convert the same.
Your next hire would eat all your profit.
Things look hard.
What if we could duplicate your team's output —
and guarantee you get a full day back per week?
52 days back per year. Or you don't pay.
That increases your conversion rate. Your productivity. Your cash flow.
It makes room for that key person your business actually needs.
If this sounds like you — click the link below and dominate with AI.

title: Nobody Wins If You Don't
setup: Behind desk at Orage HQ. Direct to camera. Slightly slower delivery.
shotDirections: Eye-level camera
Hold position — no movement
Slow delivery, generous pauses

sections.action: Direct to camera. Slight lean forward.
spokenLines: Most agencies want you to stay for three months. We don't.
We want to be partners.
The kind your business actually needs.

sections.action: Slow down on the guarantee. Land it.
spokenLines: That's why our guarantee is so strong.
A day back per week. Or you don't pay.
Because if you don't win, we don't win.
That's the whole deal.

sections.action: Hold the eye contact. Don't oversell.
spokenLines: Free 45-minute audit.
We'll show you what winning looks like in your business. Link below.

title: The Phone That Won't Stop
setup: At desk. Real phone visible on the desk. Dark wood or clean surface.
shotDirections: First shot: tight close-up on phone
Second shot: cut to you at desk
Phone needs to actually buzz on camera (timer to call yourself)

sections.action: Tight close-up on phone buzzing repeatedly. Five missed calls visible on screen. You glance at it from off-frame, look away, glance back. Phone keeps buzzing.
Cut to you at desk. Direct to camera. Slight head tilt.
spokenLines: Be honest. How many of these did you not return today?

sections.action: Hold position. Empathetic delivery. Slow on 'someone else.'
spokenLines: Every missed call is a customer someone else is going to close.
I built voice agents that pick up every call,
qualify the lead,
and book the appointment.
Your phone stops being the boss of your day.

title: What's Happening?
energy: Cinematic, dialogue-driven — different from anything else
setup: Screen showing a 'news' style chyron about a business shutting down. Dialogue between two people (you + cofounder/friend).
shotDirections: Two-shot of you + second person watching screen
Close-up insert of 'news' graphic
Phone-in-hand shot for the 'let me call my friend' beat
Final beat: cut to camera direct

sections.action: Two-shot watching a screen. Visible chyron / graphic suggesting a business closing or 'destroyed by AI.' Voice-over from the second person.
spokenLines: PERSON 2 (off): "Is that a war?"
YOU: "What are you watching?"

sections.action: Stay on the two-shot. Slight zoom in on the screen.
spokenLines: PERSON 2: "Some business got destroyed. They were strong for 5 years. I don't know what happened."
YOU: "It was AI."
PERSON 2: "What? They said they were fine. Kept doing things the same. And now they're gone."

sections.action: Cut to you direct. Then to a hypothetical viewer.
spokenLines: PERSON 2: "Damn. Is that happening to every business?"
YOU: "Only the ones not adopting AI."
YOU (to camera): "If you're doing things the same way — admin, answering emails and calls after hours, your team moving like a slow turtle — you're next."
YOU (on phone): "I have a friend who can help. Want me to call him?"
YOU (texting): "Hey, how many spots you have left?"
REPLY VISIBLE: "3."
Use AI. Click the link.

Walking parallel to camera operator
Stop at 'None of them said...' for emphasis

spokenLines: If I gave you a full day back every week — what would you actually do with it?

spokenLines: Every founder I ask gets quiet for a second. Then they get specific.
